title: GSAD-43
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Droid |type= |image=[[File:GSAD-43_GG10.jpg]] |name=GSAD-43 |homeworld= |birth= |death= |creator= |manufacturer=[[Arakyd Industries/Legends|Arakyd Industries]]''[[Cynabar's Fantastic Technology: Droids]]'' |line= |model=[[AD-series weapons maintenance droid]]''[[Galaxy Guide 10: Bounty Hunters]]'' |class= |cost= |length= |width= |height=1.5 meters |mass= |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Female programming]] |pronouns=new |sensor= |plating= |armament=*Internal [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]]/[[Missile launcher/Legends|missile launcher]] weapons pod |equipment=*[[AA-12X VerboBrain]] *Communications link with base computer *[[Repulsorlift/Legends|Repulsorlift]] unit *Sensor pod *Visual/Auditory sensors ([[Human/Legends|Human]] range) *Vocabulator speech/sound system |affiliation=*[[House Salaktori]] **[[Janna Relasi]] }} {{Quote|Are you one of ''them''?|GSAD-43|}} '''GSAD-43''' was an [[Arakyd Industries/Legends|Arakyd Industries]] [[AD-series weapons maintenance droid|AD armorer droid]] originally in the employ of [[House Salaktori]] after Arakyd failed to sell the design to the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Assigned to help [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Janna Relasi]], GSAD-43 personally bonded with her mistress. Relasi was killed while in service and, with her last words, asked the droid to capture the culprits of her [[Death/Legends|death]]. Taking the order at heart, and to the embarrassment of Salaktori, GSAD-43 started tracking [[Janna Relasi's murderer]]s through the [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlanes]] and became a bounty hunter herself. Even after capturing one of the murderers, GSAD-43 assumed that others might still be at large and, with improved weaponry and skill software, she continued her relentless task, asking her victims the ominous question: "Are you one of ''them''?" ==Biography== {{Quote|Get them for me…|Janna Relasi's last words|}} GSAD-43 was an [[AD-series weapons maintenance droid|AD armorer droid]] built by [[Arakyd Industries/Legends|Arakyd Industries]] along with several other units, on the assumption that the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] would buy their new [[Droid/Legends|droid]] design. However, after a careful analysis of the model's efficiency, Imperial authorities decided against the purchase. Arakyd Industries had already manufactured a number of AD droids and sold some of them to guilds of [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]]. GSAD-43 became a property of [[House Salaktori]], a member of the [[Bounty Hunters' Guild/Legends|Bounty Hunters' Guild]]. Salaktori assigned GSAD-43 to [[Janna Relasi]], a [[Gender/Legends|female]] bounty hunter working for the House. After working together in several hunts, Relasi came to consider GSAD-43 her old companion, and the droid was, in turn, bonded to her mistress. During one hunt, Relasi was [[Death/Legends|fatally]] wounded and barely arrived to her own [[Starship/Legends|starship]]. With her last breath, she asked GSAD-43 to catch the people who had injured her, as she would be unable to. GSAD-43 considered this to be an explicit command, albeit Relasi had failed to identify the murderers except for the fact that they were more than one. During the following weeks, GSAD-43 used the [[Computer/Legends|computer]] on [[Janna Relasi's ship|Relasi's ship]] to research on her recent activities. She also improved the firepower and tracking abilities of her hardware. Once she was ready, she tracked and eventually captured [[Janna Relasi's murderer|one of the murderers]]. This was not enough to satisfy the machine, as Relasi had specified "them", a collective, as the culprits of her death. GSAD-43 continued tracking people through [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlanes]], and looking for clues on the conspiracy that had killed her owner, with her activities and background anything but a secret to the chagrin of House Salaktori — the Guild was ashamed of the situation, as an armorer droid playing bounty hunter was laughable. During her travels, she met and impressed [[Devon Fuller]], who later inquired about the droid's past and posted about it on [[Cynabar's Droid Datalog]]. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Has anyone heard about a rogue armorer droid that used to work for House Salaktori? Apparently the blasted thing has decided it is some kind of bounty hunter now. (I laughed out loud when I heard about it.)''"
"''Don't laugh; I've actually seen it.|Grannt and Devon Fuller posting on Cynabar's Droid Datalog|}} As an armorer droid, GSAD-43 had advanced skills to repair weaponry and military gear, including [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]], [[Armor/Legends|armors]], [[Jetpack/Legends|jetpacks]] and [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlifts]]. It also had the common equipment of the AD armorer droids, including a [[Verbobrain/Legends|Verbobrain]], a [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] unit with a flight ceiling of 1 [[Meter/Legends|meter]] and a communications link. The updates she installed on herself to work as a bounty hunters included a sensor pod to increase her searching skill and an internal [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]]/[[Missile launcher/Legends|missile launcher]] weapons pod. GSAD-34 was known to ask "Are you one of ''them''?" to the individuals she captured. Although her firepower and skills were considerable, the sheer idea of a violent armorer droid was treated as a joke by some, as happened to [[Grannt]]. ==Behind the scenes== GSAD-43 was first mentioned in ''[[Galaxy Guide 10: Bounty Hunters]]'', written by [[Rick D. Stuart]]. This was also the first appearance of the AD armorer droid model. The book included an image of GSAD-43, drawn by [[David Plunkett]]. Following her role-playing stats, GSAD-43 also upgraded her skills on investigation, bureaucracy and law enforcement. ==Sources== *''[[Galaxy Guide 10: Bounty Hunters]]'' {{1stm}} *''[[Cynabar's Fantastic Technology: Droids]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=GSAD-43 }} [[Category:AD-series weapons maintenance droids]] [[Category:Bounty hunters]]
